core.action: Calculate Information Ratio as (rp - rm) / tracking_error_vol exactly — do not substitute with Excess Return / VaR or other alternatives that incorporate tail risk or change the risk normalization basis

core.action: Apply position smoothing formula new_pos = (0.92+o)/2 exactly — maintain the 0.92 coefficient that creates 50% weight on new signal and 50% on prior position; do not replace with exponential smoothing, Kalman filter, or adaptive smoothing

consequence.description: Changing the smoothing mechanism alters transaction cost profiles and signal dampening characteristics; exponential or adaptive smoothing would weight recent observations differently, causing backtest results to diverge from production behavior

core.when: When implementing percentile rank calculations for mean-reversion signal generation
core.action: Calculate percentile rank as count(values < current) / len(values) * 100 using strict 'fewer than' comparison — do not use scipy's percentileofscore with 'average' interpolation which handles ties differently

consequence.description: Using 'average' interpolation for ties produces different percentile values for discrete distributions with repeated values, causing mean-reversion signals to fire at different thresholds and generating inconsistent trading decisions
